description: In 2011, the Gillard Labor government introduced the Clean Energy Act, which established a fixed-price carbon tax to transition to an emissions trading scheme. The policy faced fierce opposition from the Coalition under Tony Abbott, leading to its repeal in 2014 after the 2013 federal election.

key_events.description: Prime Minister Julia Gillard breaks an election promise, leading to a surge in public distrust.
Tony Abbott launches the 'Axe the Tax' campaign, gaining significant media traction.
Coalition wins the 2013 federal election on a mandate to repeal the legislation.
The Senate formally votes to repeal the Clean Energy Act in July 2014.

calibration_notes: This scenario demonstrates how a major fiscal policy linked to climate change can be weaponized in political discourse, highlighting the volatility of public opinion when a policy is framed as a broken election promise versus an environmental necessity.
